fix: channel-based exfil logger — eliminate concurrent write race

Replace shared *os.File with a buffered channel (cap 256) owned by a
single writer goroutine:

- Multiple handleConn goroutines call writeExfilLog concurrently;
  concurrent WriteString on the same *os.File is not safe and could
  produce interleaved or lost log entries.

- The writer goroutine is the sole owner of the file handle; it reads
  from exfilCh and writes entries sequentially, surfacing any write
  errors to stderr rather than silently discarding them.

- On SIGTERM/SIGINT the accept loop exits, exfilCh is closed, and
  writerDone.Wait() ensures all queued detection events are flushed
  before the process exits.

- writeExfilLog uses a non-blocking select/default so a full channel
  (e.g. during an unexpected burst) logs a warning to stderr rather
  than blocking the ICAP handler goroutine.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: lazy-init india-pincode + serverExternalPackages to fix build

The `india-pincode` package reads `data/pincodes.json.gz` from disk at
init time. When `getIndiaPincode()` was called at module scope, Next.js
Turbopack evaluated it during `next build` page data collection, causing
"data file not found" because the data file wasn't in the bundled output.

Two fixes:
1. Add `serverExternalPackages: ['india-pincode']` to next.config.ts so
   the package is left as a runtime require() and can resolve its data
   file from node_modules/.
2. Make `getIndiaPincode()` lazy (called on first request, not at module
   eval time) as a defence-in-depth measure.

Implement import.source() dynamic expression for source phase imports

Add parsing, compilation, and runtime support for import.source(specifier)
which returns a Promise that resolves with the module namespace object.
This follows the same architecture as import() and import.defer():
parser recognizes the syntax, compiler emits a helper call, and the VM
dispatches to host_source_import_promise at runtime.

- Add Expr::SourceImport AST node and parser handling
- Add INTERNAL_SOURCE_IMPORT_HELPER compiler constant
- Add host_source_import_promise VM method (loads module, resolves with namespace)
- Reject new import.source(...) with SyntaxError
- Update feature probes to verify module loading works
- Remove dead code: parse_simple_module_namespace, resolve_import_specifier_path

* Fix: don't include leading :: in captured alias target name

The template registry stores alias templates without the :: prefix,
so including it in the captured name causes lookup failures during
deferred alias materialization. The class alias path has no
lookup_alias_template guard, so a ::prefixed name passes the
isValid() check but fails at materialization time.

commit.message:
fix: resolve generic TypeScript awaited function calls missing from call graph (#804)

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve generic TypeScript function callers missed by impact analysis

When a generic function call is combined with `await` (e.g. `await fn<T>(args)`),
tree-sitter-typescript parses it as a `call_expression` whose `function` field is
an `await_expression` rather than a bare `identifier`. The existing queries only
matched `call_expression { function: identifier }`, so these calls produced no
`@call.name` capture and were silently dropped from the call graph.

Fix: add two new tree-sitter query patterns to `TYPESCRIPT_QUERIES` that handle:
1. `await fn<T>(args)` — awaited generic free call
2. `await obj.fn<T>(args)` — awaited generic member call

Both patterns require the `(type_arguments)` child to be present (which is what
causes tree-sitter to parse the `function` field as an `await_expression`).
Non-generic awaited calls (`await fn(args)`) are unaffected: tree-sitter parses
them as `await_expression { call_expression { identifier } }`, which is still
captured by the existing first pattern.

Also adds a new test fixture `typescript-generic-calls` with two callers of a
generic `verifyToken<T>` function using `await` and three new integration tests.

commit.message:
fix(api): AuditLogger — non-blocking handler dispatch + file-backed persistence (#4606)

* Initial plan

* fix(api): decouple AuditLogger handler dispatch from mutex; add FileAuditLogHandler

- AuditLogger::log() now copies handlers_ under a scoped lock,
  releases the lock, then invokes each handler outside the critical
  section. Buffer append and stats update remain protected by a
  second scoped lock. This prevents slow handlers (file I/O, network
  sinks, regex matching) from stalling concurrent API threads.
- Add FileAuditLogHandler class: thread-safe JSONL-appending handler
  that opens the configured file in append mode on each write so
  entries survive process restarts.
- Add AuditLogger::addFileHandler(path) convenience method that
  registers a FileAuditLogHandler via the existing addHandler() API.
- Add persistence: section to config/audit.yaml (backend: none|file,
  file_path) so operators can enable file-backed persistence without
  code changes.
- Mark both implementation notes as [x] in FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md.

fix: address PR review comments

- test/smoke.sh: use resolvable domain (example.com) for header exfil test;
  ssl_bump requires DNS resolution before Squid can inspect inner HTTPS headers.
  Fix allowlist path to use PROJECT_ALLOWLIST_FILE variable consistently.
  Update stale DNS-failure comment.

- proxy/entrypoint.sh: remove exfil.log redirect from icap-scanner launch.
  The scanner opens exfil.log directly for detection records; redirecting
  stdout/stderr to the same file polluted it with unstructured startup logs.

- proxy/squid.conf: change exfil_fmt from %ru (full URL) to %>h (dest host).
  URL-based token detections would have recorded the token value in the log,
  contradicting the intent. Only the destination host is now persisted.

- sandboxed-copilot: proxy monitor now skips exfil.log lines that are neither
  ICAP detections nor Squid ACL entries (start with timestamp digit), preventing
  any stray scanner log from being rendered as a false EXFIL alert.

- README.md: clarify that exfil.log records the destination host, not the
  full URL or token value.

- proxy/icap-scanner/main.go: handle transient Accept() errors gracefully;
  only break on net.ErrClosed (listener shutdown); log and continue on others.

* fix: use authoritative V3 complete text to prevent cross-turn Discord message duplication

The PluginObserverBridgeService message buffer had a race condition caused
by BroadcastingSessionEventSink's fire-and-forget dispatch pattern. When
the assistant.message (complete text) event won the thread pool race and
processed before all deltas, orphaned deltas accumulated in the buffer and
contaminated the next turn's output — producing doubled/corrupt messages.

Fix: when IsCompleteMessage is true, always use the event's authoritative
complete text and discard buffered deltas. This matches what
BroadcastingSessionEventSink already does for message persistence.

Added regression test simulating the race: final processed before deltas,
orphaned deltas present when next turn arrives.

commit.message:
fix: add --ignore-installed for APT-managed unicrypto pip conflict

unicrypto is installed by Kali APT (python3-unicrypto) without a RECORD
file. pip3 install fails with 'Cannot uninstall unicrypto 0.0.10' because
it can't find the RECORD to perform the uninstall. --ignore-installed
tells pip to overwrite the APT version without trying to uninstall first.
